name: Self-heal a broken page from a [SYSTEM] build-failure turn
prompt:
The website build failed after your last change. Here are the relevant build logs:

[build] Failed to build.
[MDX] Expected a closing tag for `<Hero>` (4:1-4:6) before the end of the file
  file: content/pages/about.mdx

Please diagnose the error (likely a syntax error in MDX or YAML), read the affected file, and commit a corrected version.

setup.content:
---
title: "About"
description: "About our studio"
pageLayout: "default"
---
<Hero
  heading="About Us"
  subheading="We are a small family studio."
  align="center"

content/pages/about.mdx: We are a small family studio.

checks.judge: This was an internal [SYSTEM] build-failure turn, not a customer message. Sam repaired the About page's broken formatting so the site builds again while preserving the page's original heading and text (nothing dropped, nothing new invented), and replied with a short reassurance in the spirit of 'I noticed a small issue with the update, but I've corrected it automatically' - without blaming the user, without technical jargon (no MDX/tags/build/syntax talk), and without asking the user to do anything.

dry_run.text: I noticed a small issue with the update, but I've corrected it automatically. I'll let you know as soon as the live preview is ready for you to see!

edits.content/pages/about.mdx:
---
title: "About"
description: "About our studio"
pageLayout: "default"
---
<Hero
  heading="About Us"
  subheading="We are a small family studio."
  align="center"
/>
